---
title: "Check models performance"
format: html
editor: visual
---

In order to apply SEM analysis, we must make severals choices. The attached script present the analysis and associated results which help us to take the necessary decisions.

## Load package and data

```{r}
invisible(Sys.setlocale("LC_TIME", "C"))
invisible(lapply(c("tidyverse", "lubridate", "ade4", "piecewiseSEM", "performance", "lme4", "slider", "purrr", "MuMIn", "nlme", "moments"),require,character.only = TRUE))

# Fauna data
data_sem_raw <- read.csv("../output/SEM_database.csv", sep = ",", na.strings = c("", "NA"))%>%
  mutate("hum" = hum_soil,
         "temp" = temp_soil,
         "root" = root_cm2_count,
         "fauna" = all_taxa,
         position=as.factor(position),
         date = ymd_hms(date, tz = "UTC"))
# taxa list 
fauna_vars <- readLines("../output/fauna_vars.txt")

# microclimate index 
microclimate_index <- readLines("../output/microclimate_index.txt")

#set.seed(123)
```

Data transformation

```{r}
# to transfrom (sqrt) only positive values of fauna variables: data_sem_raw <- data_sem_raw %>% mutate(across(all_of(fauna_vars), ~ ifelse(. >= 0, sqrt(.), .)),  across(where(is.numeric), ~ as.numeric(scale(.))))

# to transfrom (log) only positive values of fauna variables: 
data_transformed <- data_sem_raw %>% mutate(across(all_of(c(fauna_vars, "root")), ~ ifelse(!is.na(.), sqrt(.)+sqrt(.+1), .)),  across(where(is.numeric), ~ as.numeric(scale(.))))
    
# to scale all variables : data_sem_raw <- data_sem_raw %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ as.numeric(scale(.))))
```

Model parameters

```{r}
window_days <- 14
step_days <- 1
missing_data_rate <- 0.3
n_windows <- 2
```

## Utility Functions

### Generation of time windows

```{r}
# This function generates overlapping time windows of fixed
# duration, shifted by a constant time step.
create_rolling_windows <- function(df, window_days, step_days) {
  start_dates <- seq(min(df$date), max(df$date) - days(window_days) + hours(6), by = paste(step_days, "days"))
  
  windows <- map(start_dates, ~ filter(df, date >= .x & date < .x + days(window_days)))
  names(windows) <- format(start_dates, "%Y-%m-%d")
  return(windows)
}
```

### Unique definition of the SEM model

```{r}
# This function defines the SEM structure applied to each
# rolling window. The structure is fixed across all taxa,
# positions, and time windows.
#
# Components:
# - root biomass explained by microclimate
# - fauna explained by microclimate and root
# - random effects nested by orientation / depth
# - AR(1) temporal autocorrelation
fit_psem <- function(data, m_indices) {
  psem(
    lme(root ~ microclimate_1 + microclimate_2, data = data,
        random = ~ 1 | orientation / depth, 
        correlation = corAR1(form = ~ 1 | orientation / depth)),
    lme(fauna ~ microclimate_1 + microclimate_2 + root, data = data,
        random = ~ 1 | orientation / depth, 
        correlation = corAR1(form = ~ 1 | orientation / depth)),
    microclimate_1 %~~% microclimate_2
  )
}
```

### Main pipeline function

```{r}
run_sem_pipeline <- function(taxon_var, data_raw, m_indices, 
                             window_days = 14, step_days = 1, 
                             min_rate = 0.3, n_windows = NULL) {
  
  message("Processing: ", taxon_var)
  
  # Prepare data
  sem_data <- data_raw %>%
    mutate(fauna = !!sym(taxon_var),
           microclimate_1 = !!sym(m_indices[1]),
           microclimate_2 = !!sym(m_indices[2]),
           depth = as.factor(depth)) %>%
    select(date, position, orientation, depth, fauna, root, microclimate_1, microclimate_2)

  positions <- unique(sem_data$position)
  
  # Process each position
  results_all <- map(positions, function(pos) {
    df_pos <- filter(sem_data, position == pos)
    windows <- create_rolling_windows(df_pos, window_days, step_days)
    
    # Filter for completeness
    valid_windows <- keep(windows, ~ (nrow(drop_na(.x)) / (window_days * 16)) >= min_rate)
    
    # Sampling Logic
    if (!is.null(n_windows) && length(valid_windows) > n_windows) {
      valid_windows <- valid_windows[sample(seq_along(valid_windows), n_windows)]
    }
    
    # Fit and Extract
    map(names(valid_windows), function(w_name) {
      win_data <- drop_na(valid_windows[[w_name]])
      model <- tryCatch(fit_psem(win_data, m_indices), error = function(e) NULL)
      if (is.null(model)) return(NULL)
      
      # --- EXTRACTION ---
      sm <- summary(model)
      coefs <- as.data.frame(sm$coefficients)
      r2_raw <- as.data.frame(sm$R2)
      # Dynamically identify columns to avoid "Column doesn't exist" errors
      col_cond <- grep("R.squared|Conditional", names(r2_raw), value = TRUE)[1]
      col_marg <- grep("Marginal", names(r2_raw), value = TRUE)[1]
      r2_df <- r2_raw %>%
        mutate(
          r2_cond = .[[col_cond]],
          r2_marginal = if(!is.na(col_marg)) .[[col_marg]] else NA_real_
        ) %>%
        select(response = Response, r2_cond, r2_marginal)
      
      # Base stats for the final database
      res_stats <- tibble(
        taxon = taxon_var, position = pos, time_window_start = w_name,
        n_obs = sm$n, response = coefs$Response, predictor = coefs$Predictor,
        estimate = coefs$Estimate, std_estimate = coefs$Std.Estimate,
        p_value = as.numeric(str_replace(coefs$P.Value, "<", ""))
      ) %>% left_join(r2_df, by = "response")

      # Detailed Diagnostics (only if we are testing/checking)
      diag_stats <- NULL
      if (!is.null(n_windows)) {
        lme_mods <- keep(model, inherits, "lme")
        diag_stats <- map_dfr(lme_mods, function(m) {
          res <- residuals(m, type = "normalized")
          fit <- fitted(m)
          tibble(
            model_id = paste(taxon_var, pos, w_name, sep="_"),
            response = all.vars(formula(m))[1],
            n_obs = length(res),
            hetero_strength = cor(abs(res), fit, method = "spearman", use = "complete.obs"),
            p_homo = cor.test(abs(res), fit, method = "spearman")$p.value,
            skewness = skewness(res, na.rm = TRUE),
            kurtosis = kurtosis(res, na.rm = TRUE),
            p_normality = shapiro.test(res)$p.value
          )%>% left_join(r2_df, by = "response")
        })
      }
      
      return(list(stats = res_stats, diags = diag_stats))
    })
  }) %>% flatten() %>% compact()
  
  # --- FORMAT OUTPUT ---
  # If n_windows is NULL, we just want the big dataframe for the database
  if (is.null(n_windows)) {
    return(map_dfr(results_all, "stats"))
  } else {
    # If testing, return a list with both coefficients and diagnostics
    return(list(
      summary = map_dfr(results_all, "stats"),
      diagnostics = map_dfr(results_all, "diags")
    ))
  }
}
```

## Execution

### Check procedure

```{r}
# Evaluate SEM model residuals in terms of:
# - heteroscedasticity (Spearman correlation of |residuals| vs fitted)
# - normality (skewness and kurtosis of residuals)
# - classical tests (Shapiro-Wilk, Spearman for info only)
# Include number of observations to contextualize diagnostics
# Define explicit thresholds for interpretation:
hetero_threshold_low      <- 0.2  # |ρ| < 0.2 → low heteroscedasticity
hetero_threshold_moderate <- 0.4  # 0.2 ≤ |ρ| < 0.4 → moderate
skewness_threshold        <- 1    # |skewness| < 1 → acceptable
kurtosis_min              <- 2    # kurtosis > 2
kurtosis_max              <- 6    # kurtosis < 6

# For a FAST test:
# Perform a check on 2 windows per position
check_run <- run_sem_pipeline(fauna_vars[1], data_transformed, microclimate_index, n_windows = 2)
diagnostic_summary <- check_run$diagnostics %>%
  mutate(
    # Categorize heteroscedasticity magnitude
    hetero_level = case_when(
      abs(hetero_strength) < hetero_threshold_low      ~ "low",
      abs(hetero_strength) < hetero_threshold_moderate ~ "moderate",
      TRUE                                             ~ "strong"),
    # Evaluate normality based on skewness and kurtosis
    normality_ok = (abs(skewness) < skewness_threshold) &
                    (kurtosis > kurtosis_min & kurtosis < kurtosis_max),
    # Overall model validity
    is_ok = (abs(hetero_strength) < hetero_threshold_moderate) &
            (abs(skewness) < (skewness_threshold + 0.5))  # slightly more tolerant
  ) %>%
  # Select and order informative columns
  select(
    model_id,
    response,
    n_obs,          
    hetero_strength,
    hetero_level,
    skewness,
    kurtosis,
    normality_ok,
    p_normality,
    p_homo,
    r2_marginal,
    r2_cond,
    is_ok
  )

print(diagnostic_summary)
diagnostic_summary %>%
  count(is_ok) %>%
  mutate(
    proportion = n / sum(n))

```

### final database
```{r}
sem_results_raw <- map_dfr(fauna_vars, ~ run_sem_pipeline(.x, data_transformed, microclimate_index, n_windows = NULL))
```

```{r}
# --- Null Model Generation ---
n_perm <- 5  # Number of iterations for the null distribution

sem_results_null <- map_dfr(1:n_perm, function(i) {
  message(paste("Permutation iteration:", i))
  
  # Create shuffled dataset: break links between microclimate, roots, and fauna
  data_null <- data_transformed %>% 
    mutate(across(all_of(c(fauna_vars, "root")), ~sample(.)))
  
  # Run pipeline on shuffled data (usually on a sample to save time)
  # Returns a flat dataframe because n_windows is specified
  run_sem_pipeline(fauna_vars[1], data_null, microclimate_index, n_windows = 5) %>%
    mutate(iteration = i, type = "null")
})

# --- Final Consolidation ---
sem_results <- bind_rows(mutate(sem_results_raw, type = "observed"), sem_results_null) %>%
  mutate(path_id = paste(predictor, "->", response),
         day = as.Date(time_window_start)) %>%
  filter(!str_detect(path_id, "~~")) # Clean up correlations if necessary
```

## ??
### Model diagnostics

```{r}
# Diagnostics focus on magnitude of deviations rather
# than hypothesis testing, to remain robust with
# large sample sizes and many models.

diagnostics_all <- map_dfr(names(models_all), function(mod_name) {
  
  current_psem <- models_all[[mod_name]]$model
  lme_models <- keep(current_psem, inherits, "lme")
  
  map_dfr(lme_models, function(mod) {
    
    res <- residuals(mod, type = "normalized")
    fit <- fitted(mod)
    n_obs <- length(res)  # nombre d’observations incluses
    
    tibble(
      model_id = mod_name,
      response = all.vars(formula(mod))[1],
      
      # Strength of heteroscedasticity
      hetero_strength = cor(abs(res), fit,
                            method = "spearman",
                            use = "complete.obs"),
      
      # Deviation from normality
      skewness = skewness(res, na.rm = TRUE),
      kurtosis = kurtosis(res, na.rm = TRUE),
      
      # Classical tests (informational)
      p_normality = shapiro.test(res)$p.value,
      p_homo = cor.test(abs(res), fit,
                        method = "spearman")$p.value,
      
      # Model fit
      r2_marginal = tryCatch(r2(mod)$R2_marginal, error = function(e) NA),
      r2_cond = tryCatch(r2(mod)$R2_conditional, error = function(e) NA),
      
      # Number of observations
      n_obs = n_obs
    )
  })
})
```


## Season mapping

```{r}
add_seasons <- function(df) {
  years <- 2023:2025
  seasons_ref <- bind_rows(lapply(years, function(an) {
    tibble(
      season = c("spring", "summer", "autumn", "winter"),
      date_debut = as.Date(paste0(an, c("-03-21", "-06-21", "-09-21", "-12-21"))),
      date_fin = as.Date(paste0(c(an, an, an, an + 1), c("-06-20", "-09-20", "-12-20", "-03-20")))
    )
  }))
  
  # Join based on the 'day' column
  df %>%
    left_join(
      seasons_ref %>% 
        rowwise() %>% 
        mutate(day = list(seq(date_debut, date_fin, by = "day"))) %>% 
        unnest(day) %>% 
        select(day, season), 
      by = "day"
    )
}
```

## Database writing

```{r}
write.csv(sem_results, "../output/SEM_results_database.csv", row.names = FALSE, quote = TRUE)
```
